title: Buffer Overflow, Underflow, and Copy Boundaries · Invariant Recall
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the bounded frame transformation contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for bounded frame transformation after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive half-open ranges and prove start, count, end, and terminator relationships for changed empty and maximum cases.
Validate both readable source and writable destination extents before memcpy-like operations.
Choose memcpy only for proven non-overlap and memmove for overlapping byte ranges.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Fortify Runtime Limits
steps.instruction:
review case — A review native-systems team handles memory case 3014. Produce bounded C evidence to move overlapping bytes inside proven bounds; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat fortified libraries and object-size checks as defense layers with optimization and visibility limits.
